title: Jerijador

{{Quote|Hypersignal sensor data and recalculation indicates that all ships shall emerge from hyperspace in formation. Only the strike cruiser ''Shador'' is anticipated to have deviated more than an arc second since we left Jerijador.|Captain Oweg, Imperial warship ''Valiant''|<ref name="The Full Might of the Empire">{{WEGCite|story=The Full Might of the Empire|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}}</ref>}}
'''Jerijador''', located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] teeming with a variety of lifeforms that was hospitable to [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warship/Legends|warships]] led by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vensell]] staged at Jerijador in preparation for an assault on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. This attracted many [[Scavenger/Legends|scavengers]] who came to salvage the junk that the departing Imperial [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] left behind upon entering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].

==Description==
A terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]] of landmasses and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], Jerijador teemed with many different kinds of life and was quite hospitable to [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]].<ref name="Rebellion" /> The planet was located in the [[Jerijador system/Legends|Jerijador system]] of the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]],<ref name="Atlas Online Companion" /> within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion<ref name="Atlas" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Rebellion" />

==History==
Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]],<ref name="Imperial Sourcebook">[[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warships]]<ref name="A Salvager's Run">{{WEGCite|story=A Salvager's Run|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}}</ref> under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vensell]] staged at Jerijador before embarking on an attack against [[Alliance Military/Legends|Rebel forces]]. The fleet included the warship [[Valiant (starship)|''Valiant'']], commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Oweg]], and the [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruiser]] [[Shador (Strike-class)|''Shador'']].<ref name="The Full Might of the Empire" />

Once the Imperial warships departed Jerijador by jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], more than a dozen [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]]—including ''[[The Lucky Find]]'', crewed by Captain [[Sreethyn]] and hireling [[Kanda Farral]]—raced from the nearby planet to collect the refuse that the fleet had left behind. With the assistance of four [[Salvage droid/Legends|retrieval droids]], Farral entered the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] over Jerijador wearing a [[Gravity/Legends|zero-gravity]] [[Environment suit/Legends|work suit]] and began claiming the Empire's discarded goods for Sreethyn's scavenging business.<ref name="A Salvager's Run" />

By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] to [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].</ref> Jerijador exported cheap shoe kits. One such lot had made its way to the planet [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]] during the [[Ismaren plot|''Eye of Palpatine'' incident]].<ref name="Children of the Jedi" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jerijador was created by author [[Greg Gorden]] for the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', originally published by [[West End Games]] in [[October]] [[1989]] as a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The ''Imperial Sourcebook'' mentioned Jerijador in the short companion narratives "[[A Salvager's Run]]" and "[[The Full Might of the Empire]]."<ref name="A Salvager's Run" /><ref name="The Full Might of the Empire" />

Jerijador was later one of 200 playable worlds included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which provided the planet's only visual depiction, an image also used to illustrate eleven other planets of similar composition in the game.<ref name="Rebellion" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]], placed the Jerijador [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square T-9.<ref name="Atlas" />

===''Star Wars: Rebellion'' cartography===
''Star Wars: Rebellion'' provides hyperspace travel [[Time/Legends|times]] between each planet in the game. However, in several instances these times are significantly longer than those in other [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] sources, which seems to reflect the ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' hyperspace times as game mechanics rather than actual figures. Jerijador's intra-[[Sector/Legends|sector]] hyperspace times and distance from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] according to ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' using the game's fastest [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] are as follows. Note that this chart only includes planets that were [[canon]]ically located in the Calaron sector with respect to the Legends continuity, excepting Coruscant.
